review_title: The future of automation
description: Excellent customer service, a dedicated [sensitive content hidden], this will replace Zapier entirely one day.
author: Roy A.
pros: The ability to do anything you can think of.

author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: Massive Potential, But Needs a Lot of Work
description: My experience with Cheat Layer has been frustrating, but with tremendous potential still waiting to be tapped into. That's why I would recommend it. The developer has had huge success because of the potential that it offers individuals and businesses. However, if you're not a coder, you're going to struggle with it, despite the advertising saying that it's for non-coders. The technology is still nascent, and it doesn't work consistently, or at all, and this will be frustrating for people who don't know how to code. But it will likely get better in time.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: Groundbreaking software that allows you to manipulate websites in a way that has never been done before. Near endless potential for money-making ideas.
cons: The software promises the moon, but at the moment it under delivers. It advertises that you can do anything with its no-code automation software, but the code that it generates tends to only work on rare occasions, if at all. You still need to be a JavaScript developer to understand it, edit it, or get it to work properly. Granted that it's still early in its development, but it doesn't exactly deliver on the promise made in its advertisement. There's a big learning curve and you will spend a huge amount of time struggling to get the code that it generates to work properly. Now, if you are a JavaScript developer, then there's enormous potential here. So you have to know this going in. There's also huge room for improvement and new features being added every month, so investing in it now may be a good long-term strategy that will pay for itself moving forward.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: Cheat Layer Automation and web scraping
author: Bruce M.
pros: I have been using Cheat Layer for about 1 year, during which I have had the opportunity to attend a bootcamp, work one-one with one of their office hour software engineers and talk to [SENSITIVE CONTENT] himself. Every interaction has been excellent. The dedication and hard work the team puts into this is outstanding.As far as Cheat Layer itself.. it is a very flexible tool. I have used it mainly for scraping websites and populating Google Sheets. There are many no-code “cheats” that you can use for many different situations/solutions, and you can have GPT-3 create code for you based on your “natural language” query and then add to this yourself. A keyboard/action recorder enables almost any set of actions to be taken on a website - part of which is a feature where elements that change, like buttons, will be correctly spotted-whether they change in name, position or both.I like the ability to sell the solutions I create via the white-label option.
cons: I can’t think of anything: it is a product that meets many needs and looks to have a bright future. If I had to state something, Cheat Layer no code can be improved but I see that as on the "continually improving" side especially with the upcoming Project Atlas.
author_duration: Used the software for: 1-2 years

review_title: Great tool for power-users
description: Take more than a few minutes when researching this product and if you have the patience to learn this, you can do some pretty impressive things without writing code.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: First thing to understand is that Cheat Layer is a platform more than a product. I love it, and love what it can do. It's a no-code tool that has the potential to do more great things than I first imagined. For the right users (semi-technical business-types and above), it's value is in it's power, not easy of use. There is some remarkable flexibility and a remarkable future ahead as the team rolls out new improvements. They seem to do that regularly the product has a bright future.
cons: CL is still in the early days, so don't expect a perfectly polished product. The UI is ugly and very clunky (almost awful) so you'll need to overlook that. And there is a learning curve, but it's value and potential isn't always easy to understand or learn.
author_duration: Used the software for: 6-12 months

review_title: First-Time User
description: The customer service is on point and I think this is what will make you acquire and keep new client regardless of the rather hard to use software (well at least for a beginner that hasn't used your software really much). This is why I think when you want to onboard new clients that may not be expert in the automation industry you need to really support them and be there to answer their questions. Also, you should really focus and analyze the questions that beginners ask because this is a gold mine for content such as Youtube videos. Questions that new users ask should be what you make videos about.
author: CharlesHenri R.
pros: All the different integrations. Action recorder is really great to save time and if you don't really know which function to choose
cons: It's still not clear what each function does, I'd add some more details for the new user. It would be quicker then to have to check each time in your Youtube videos. Other then this I still have not used the tool enough to tell
author_duration: Used the software for: I used a free trial

review_title: Definitely worth it
description: So far I can see Cheat Layer being a very important part of my business. I can see that I can integrate very well with almost any other programs I use which is really cool and time saving. The support is awesome and I really like the product.
author: Ashley D.
pros: I love that this software can automate almost any repetitive process. I am still learning how it works, but [SENSITIVE CONTENT] and the onboarding totally sold me. How many other softwares offer a LIVE one on one training when you first start using a software? That to me is priceless and I can't wait to do more with Cheat Layer.
cons: The onboarding is a bit unclear. I didn't see the "next" button at all at first and there are no shortcuts labeled for Mac users. But this was all cleared up during my live onboarding. I am having some issues with moving between tabs and getting the extension to work, but hopefully support can help with that.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

description: I've been using Cheat Layer for over 6 months now and it has become an integral part of my workflow. I really appreciate the natural language commands that Cheat Layer offers - they cut down on the time I spend on tedious tasks by at least 50%. The platform does have a steep learning curve. The support is great though. The [SENSITIVE CONTENT] answers a ton of questions on the Facebook group, organises bootcamps and also holds office hours.

pros: The GPT-3 powered platform is a great way to automate repetitive browser tasks and web scraping. It uses natural language and converts it into code. This is a great way to save time and automate tasks on the internet.
cons: The software has steep learning curve but the support is superb. The [SENSITIVE CONTENT] has a Facebook group as well as office hours to enable support.

review_title: RPA is easy with Cheat Layer
description: RPA or automation things will definitely replace some of our works in near future. Learning them in advance is a worthy investment in oneself.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: UI/UX: definitely it is UI/UX. You have no problem in using the software. What really matters would be to map out logic flow. It's like making ppt. You know how to open and use ppt, but you may not be good at making quality ppt. It's the same thing. Software itself is very user-friendly, but drafting out quality logic flow may take some time. But there are a couple of pre-made templates you can refer to from the website.
cons: Desktop app: not yet an official version. But the team said that the official one will be released soon. It's a bit unstable, not really critical though. Comparing to browser ver., desktop ver. should enhance its usability further.
